The father didn't support his acting career, but the job as a waiter and cab driver was part of his attempts to make a living while pursuing acting. However, the father's quote is about him getting a job, not specifically disapproving of the bartender job. So maybe the error here is that he wasn't a bartender but a waiter/cab driver. The passage details his struggles in the 70s, moving to Hollywood, financial difficulties, and then after his brother's death, he started writing, eventually leading to success with Crash. So ups and downs are definitely mentioned. Option (C): "His brother's death inspired his writing Half-Deserted Streets." The passage states that after his brother's murder, he started writing as a way to explore the pain of Hell's Kitchen, and that the play was based on his brother's killing. So this is true.
